genre_refs: 'Bus window view' / 'Optimist vs Pessimist on the bus' cartoon meme (artist Genildo Ronchi)
description: A well-known internet 'bus window' comparison cartoon repurposed as a Department of Labor meme. The illustration (signed 'Genildo' bottom right) depicts the interior of a bus with two passengers. On the left, a sad, gray-faced man in a purple shirt slumps in his seat, staring at a window that shows only a bleak gray rock wall; bold white impact-style caps caption him 'FUNDING UZBEKISTAN'S COTTON INDUSTRY.' On the right, a cheerful, smiling man in an orange/red shirt sits upright gazing out his window at a bright, sunny landscape of green hills, a winding river, and a glowing sunset; he is captioned 'INVESTING IN AMERICAN WORKERS.' The classic meme format contrasts a pessimistic, dreary view with an optimistic, beautiful one to argue that the same situation depends on perspective/choice. The propaganda technique uses a familiar humorous meme template to ridicule foreign-aid spending (funding overseas industry) versus the favored policy of domestic investment, making an 'America First' budget argument in a shareable, lighthearted package aimed at a general social-media audience.
notable_text: FUNDING UZBEKISTAN'S COTTON INDUSTRY | INVESTING IN AMERICAN WORKERS
